steps.title: Translate rates and times into distances
steps.content:
Distance = rate × time.
Rhett drives at 35 mph for r hours $\Rightarrow$ distance = 35r miles.
Jessica drives at 40 mph for j hours $\Rightarrow$ distance = 40j miles.
Total distance = 35r + 40j miles.

steps.title: Apply the total distance condition
steps.content:
The problem states the total distance was under 220 miles, so the total must be strictly less than 220:
$$35r + 40j \lt 220$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — '35r + 40j > 220': Uses the correct coefficients but the wrong inequality direction — this would mean the total exceeds 220 miles. ✗
  • Choice B — '35r + 40j < 220': Rhett’s distance (35r) plus Jessica’s distance (40j) is less than 220 miles — exactly the given constraint. ✓
  • Choice C — '40r + 35j > 220': Swaps the speeds (assigns 40 to Rhett and 35 to Jessica) and uses the wrong inequality direction. ✗
  • Choice D — '40r + 35j < 220': Has the correct inequality direction but swaps Rhett’s and Jessica’s speeds. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
